metadata.title: Composite Site Productivity Index (CSPI) analytical chain v1.0.0 for the conterminous United States

creators.name: Weiskittel, Aaron R.
creators.affiliation: University of Maine, Center for Research on Sustainable Forests

metadata.description:
Companion analytical chain deposit for the Composite Site Productivity Index (CSPI) data release at Zenodo concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20515034. This deposit contains the manuscript drafts, intermediate result tables, pipeline scripts, outreach materials, and documentation supporting the v0.10g manuscript "Beyond site index: FIA's own classification system disagrees with site index but agrees with a multi-dimensional composite of forest productivity at 30 m across the conterminous United States" (in revision at Forest Ecology and Management).

Key empirical findings:

  • FIA's seven-class site productivity classification (SITECLCD) tracks biomass growth (BGI alone OOB R² = 0.808) more closely than site index (ESI alone OOB R² = 0.751).
  • Three independent traditional site index measurements (raw FIA SICOND, NRCS SSURGO coforprod, Cieszewski-Bailey GADA refit) all carry moderate biomass-growth structure; the raw SICOND × ESI = -0.08 correlation is a base-age Simpson paradox.
  • A hierarchical EPA Level I/II/III ecoregion-weighted composite paired with the equal-weight composite increases SITECLCD recovery from OOB R² = 0.812 to 0.832 (+0.020, 95% CI non-overlapping).

Includes multidim_v3, v4, v5_L3, v5_ML, and v5_ML2 result CSVs, the corresponding R and Python pipeline scripts, the v0.10g manuscript draft plus the FEM combined submission docx (4 MB with 11 figures), four-document outreach package, and stress test plus reorganization documentation. Source repository at holoros/cspi-conus.
